subject: Molecule test for infrastructure playbook.
commit_message:
Molecule test for infrastructure playbook.

These tests execute the whole infrastructure playbook.

Issue-ID: OOM-1816
Change-Id: I793191e05cb0b527c3da6d820c1916231e69d865
Signed-off-by: Samuli Silvius <s.silvius@partner.samsung.com>
Signed-off-by: Michal Zegan <m.zegan@samsung.com>

Patch Set 11:

After rebasing on all recent changes it still fails on

    fatal: [infrastructure-server]: FAILED! => {"attempts": 30, "changed": false, "msg": "Failed to connect to nexus.infrastructure-server at port 443: [Errno 111] Connection refused"}
    
    PLAY RECAP *********************************************************************
    infrastructure-server      : ok=70   changed=38   unreachable=0    failed=1

Samuli did you do any investigation on that? AFAIR even previously we encountered that issue with connecting to nexus.

Patch Set 11:

(1 comment)

whow, this is a big test :-) I checked just briefly and need to come back to this but it looks like completely new installer. I have some concerns that keeping this living and aligned with tested code will effectively double maintenance efforts.

Patch Set 11:

> Patch Set 11:
> 
> (1 comment)
> 
> whow, this is a big test :-) I checked just briefly and need to come back to this but it looks like completely new installer. I have some concerns that keeping this living and aligned with tested code will effectively double maintenance efforts.

this is the first time you checked this:)

Tests are not something we drag behind as additional effort. Molecule test are automated and developer get feedback. And developer will run it already at own laptop before pushing. But latest the commit will run it. 

You should be more concerned when people can push commits into repo ("easily without effort") without any test gating it. And then team need to spend a huge hours to test all constantly manually. Feedback will be long.

And this playbook level test is just a collection of role level tests, there is no code duplication. I.e. molecule test for individual roles uses same code. 
In this commit just happens to have many updates to also to those individual roles. Not all files in this commit are just for this playbook level test.

Patch Set 12:

> Patch Set 11:
> 
> After rebasing on all recent changes it still fails on
> 
>     fatal: [infrastructure-server]: FAILED! => {"attempts": 30, "changed": false, "msg": "Failed to connect to nexus.infrastructure-server at port 443: [Errno 111] Connection refused"}
>     
>     PLAY RECAP *********************************************************************
>     infrastructure-server      : ok=70   changed=38   unreachable=0    failed=1
> 
> Samuli did you do any investigation on that? AFAIR even previously we encountered that issue with connecting to nexus.

I just tried, but it failed for me to same. I don't know what broke it, it was working for me previously. I don't have time now to back to deep investigation, so disabled now that images pushing (it's not tested by default in real env either e.g. nightly). So somebody could even manually test it in real env, maybe it's broken (but more likely related to molecule) as nightly does not test it.

Patch Set 13:

Hello, I take this change over with samuli's consent.

Patch Set 14:

This patchset is a *major* change so please review from scratch.
For example now the original/real infrastructure.yml playbook is being used directly.
Also this test uses dind, and even has one verifier test that
verifies if kubernetes nodes have nexus access.
Ofc if the converge playbook finished, infra already does, and no need to verify this.
I ran these tests locally, they passed.
There is one interesting caveat, chrony will probably fiddle with host system time, these molecule test containers do have enough privileges in the system for changing time. Would be nice to fix this somehow.

Patch Set 15:

(5 comments)

Why docker images are still downloaded with host docker engine in prepare- roles? If dind can it be also done on infra docker?

Should prepare-play-infrastructure role functionality be in prepare-nexus role instead?

Patch Set 15:

(2 comments)

> Patch Set 15:
> 
> (5 comments)
> 
> Why docker images are still downloaded with host docker engine in prepare- roles? If dind can it be also done on infra docker?
No. Part of infra playbook is installation of docker, so you don't expect docker to be there at the time the playbook is imported. And especially you do not expect it to be started. That is why I call host docker, because we have a guarantee to have docker in the host *already*. The only requirement is for the user running ansible to be in docker group, but that seems to be met even for the molecule-dev docker thing, so no problem.
> 
> Should prepare-play-infrastructure role functionality be in prepare-nexus role instead?
Ned to check, likely yes.

Patch Set 15:

(1 comment)

> Patch Set 15:
> 
> (2 comments)
> 
> > Patch Set 15:
> > 
> > (5 comments)
> > 
> > Why docker images are still downloaded with host docker engine in prepare- roles? If dind can it be also done on infra docker?
> No. Part of infra playbook is installation of docker, so you don't expect docker to be there at the time the playbook is imported. And especially you do not expect it to be started. That is why I call host docker, because we have a guarantee to have docker in the host *already*. The only requirement is for the user running ansible to be in docker group, but that seems to be met even for the molecule-dev docker thing, so no problem.
> > 
> > Should prepare-play-infrastructure role functionality be in prepare-nexus role instead?
> Ned to check, likely yes.

Oh yes of course, yes need to use host docker.
